title: polypodium paucinervatum
intro: Polypodium paucinervatum is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as a type of leathery-leaved or shield fern. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Polypodium paucinervatum include its ability to aid in the healing of cuts and burns, reduce inflammation and swelling, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Polypodium paucinervatum is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
